message:
fix(io): RAII polling guard for events buffer and mark deregistered on retry failure

PollingGuard: Wrap the events buffer taken from the driver in a
drop guard that restores it on unwind. Previously, if reactor.poll()
panicked, the events buffer would be lost (leaked from the driver's
perspective), and subsequent polls would allocate a fresh buffer each
time—degrading to unbounded allocation under repeated panics. The
guard also moves events ownership tracking out of manual code flow,
making the take→poll→restore sequence exception-safe.

Deregistration: When deregister() fails and the retry also fails,
now sets self.deregistered = true before returning the original error.
Previously, the failed retry left deregistered=false, causing the
Drop impl to attempt deregistration again—which would also fail,
potentially logging misleading errors and leaving a stale registration
in the reactor that could receive spurious events for a reused fd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
